name: Ohio Sour Cherry Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. For the Crisco crust, combine flour and salt in a food processor.
Cut frozen Crisco shortening into chunks with a knife and add to the food processor.
Process (pulse) until the mixture forms fine crumbs.
2. Add ice water while the machine is running and continue to run the processor until dough forms on the blades.
3. Remove and form the dough into two balls. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
4. Preheat oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. For the Sour Cherry Filling, combine brown sugar and white sugar, cinnamon, and cornstarch in a saucepan.
6. Add cherry juice and cook until the mixture is thick and bubbly.
7. Stir and boil the mixture for one minute.
8. Add cherries and cook for one minute or until it starts to boil again.
9. Remove from heat and add Crisco shortening, almond extract, and vanilla extracts.
10. Remove refrigerated dough.
11. On a lightly floured surface, roll the bottom crust into a circle that is 1/8-inch thick and about 1 ½ inches larger than the inverted pie plate.
12. Gently, ease the dough into the pie plate without stretching it. Trim the edge even with the pie plate.
13. Spoon the cherry filling into the prepared pastry.
14. Roll the top crust the same way as the bottom.
15. Brush the top crust with milk and sprinkle lightly with sugar.
16. Bake at 425 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 minutes. Reduce temperature to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for 25 minutes or until the crust is golden brown.
Serves 6 to 8.
